title: BMSSP in Isabelle/HOL: an unconditional, non-vacuous size-parametric runtime bound
description: A machine-checked Isabelle/HOL development of the Bounded Multi-Source Shortest Path (BMSSP) recurrence, the recursive engine of the Duan-Mao-Mao-Shu-Yin (2025) sub-sorting-barrier directed single-source shortest-path algorithm. The development is layered: an abstract semantic correctness layer with an executable shortest-distance function; an amortized cost model over an abstract Insert/BatchPrepend/Pull partition interface realized by a bucketed data structure; and a size-parametric layer over an unbounded graph family. Its headline result is an assumption-free, non-vacuous, size-parametric running-time theorem: the charged running time on an unbounded unit-weight directed path family is O(|V| * (ln |V|)^(2/3)) in the genuine graph size, with no hypotheses, on a family whose size tends to infinity and for which a complete run provably exists. The development contains no sorry, oops, axiomatization, consts, or SMT oracle, and is verified by a clean Isabelle2025-2 build importing only HOL and HOL-Library.

creators.name: de Queiroz, Ruy J. G. B.
creators.affiliation: Centro de Informática, Universidade Federal de Pernambuco
